heading: HHS valvular heart disease activities
label: 285b–9 HHS valvular heart disease activities
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Cardiovascular Advances in Research and Opportunities Legacy Act, and not as part of the Public Health Service Act which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary of Health and Human Services (referred to in this section as the “Secretary”) shall, as appropriate, continue activities related to research, education, and awareness of valvular heart diseases.

children.heading: NIH research related to valvular heart diseases
children.label: b NIH research related to valvular heart diseases

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director of the National Institutes of Health, in consultation with the Director of the National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institute, may support or conduct research regarding valvular heart diseases.

children.label: A
children.text: Use of advanced technological imaging and other relevant methods to generate data related to valvular heart diseases.

children.label: B
children.text: Assessing potential risk factors for sudden cardiac arrest or sudden cardiac death from valvular heart diseases.

children.label: C
children.text: Other activities, as appropriate, in order to improve the availability of information on, and advance research related to, valvular heart diseases.

children.heading: Mitral valve prolapse workshop
children.label: 3 Mitral valve prolapse workshop
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Director of the National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institute shall, as appropriate, convene a workshop composed of subject matter experts and stakeholders to identify research needs and opportunities to develop recommendations for the identification and treatment of individuals with mitral valve prolapse, including such individuals who may be at risk for sudden cardiac arrest or sudden cardiac death.December 20, 2022

children.heading: Prevention activities to improve awareness of sudden cardiac death as a result of valvular heart diseases
children.label: c Prevention activities to improve awareness of sudden cardiac death as a result of valvular heart diseases
A
children.text: award grants or contracts to public or nonprofit private entities to carry out activities under this subsection; and

children.label: B
children.text: directly, or through grants or contracts, provide technical assistance with respect to such activities.

children.label: A
children.text: continuing activities at the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention related to valvular heart diseases;

children.label: B
children.text: improving the awareness of the public concerning any risk factors for, the symptoms of, and the public health impact of, valvular heart diseases; and

children.label: C
children.text: enhancing public health data collection and improving the quality of such data, as appropriate, regarding cardiac arrests, including cardiac arrests that occur outside of the hospital.

children.heading: Grant prioritization
children.label: 3 Grant prioritization
children.text: The Secretary may, in awarding grants or entering into contracts pursuant to paragraph (1), give priority to entities seeking to carry out projects for populations most impacted by valvular heart diseases.

children.heading: Coordination of activities
children.label: 4 Coordination of activities
children.text: The Secretary shall, as appropriate, ensure that activities under this section are coordinated with other agencies and offices of the Department of Health and Human Services that carry out activities regarding valvular heart diseases.

children.heading: Best practices
children.label: 5 Best practices
children.text: The Secretary shall, as applicable and appropriate, identify and disseminate best practices for relevant health care providers related to valvular heart diseases.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: d Authorization of appropriations
children.text: For purposes of carrying out this section, there are authorized to be appropriated $28,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2023 through 2027.
